title: Intensely Buttery Mexican Padron Peppers Tostada
description: A rich and velvety Mexican dish featuring padron peppers, perfect for a date night, this jain-friendly tostada is cured with yuzu for a unique flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roast the padron peppers in the oven for 20 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool, then peel off the skin and remove the seeds.
In a large skillet, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the roasted padron peppers to the skillet and stir to coat with the butter.
Squeeze the yuzu juice over the peppers and stir to combine.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the tostadas by spooning the buttery padron peppers onto a tortilla and topping with salt, black pepper, and chopped cilantro.
Serve the tostadas with a lime wedge on the side.
